nodes.label: U.S. and Israel launch pre-emptive strikes on Iran
Iran vows to attack any ship attempting to transit the Strait of Hormuz
Middle East oil and gas shipping costs surge to all-time high
Container lines pause/avoid Suez and Bab el-Mandeb; reroute via Cape of Good Hop
Saudi Aramco shuts Ras Tanura refinery after drone strike (source)
Qatar LNG supply disrupted amid regional strikes
Israeli oil and gas fields down due to regional strikes
Oil rises as expanding US-Israeli conflict with Iran elevates supply risks
Global energy costs soar as Iran crisis disrupts shipping and production
Marine insurers cancel war-risk cover as Iran conflict intensifies
OPEC+ agrees modest oil output boost even as conflict disrupts shipments
~10% of global container fleet caught in Hormuz backup (shipping CEO)
Asian countries reliant on Middle East supply face oil disruption risks
India reduces gas supply to industries after Qatar outage (sources)
Iran war enters fourth day as markets slide

edges.label: triggers_retaliatory_threat
edges.why: Reuters reports the direct strikes preceded Iran's warnings against Hormuz transit as the conflict escalated.

edges.label: drives
edges.why: Threat/closure risk increases war-risk premia and reduces available tonnage, pushing freight rates higher.

edges.label: amplifies
edges.why: Loss of cover and higher premiums make voyages uneconomic for some owners, tightening supply of ships.

edges.label: increases_risk
edges.why: Hormuz is a critical chokepoint for crude and LNG; credible closure risk lifts the oil risk premium.

edges.label: increases_risk
edges.why: Refinery outages and vulnerability of Gulf infrastructure add to supply/product disruption risk.

edges.label: pressures
edges.why: Higher oil prices worsen trade balances and inflation for net importers, often weakening EM FX.

edges.label: widens
edges.why: Energy-driven inflation risk can lift yields/term premia and widen credit spreads during geopolitical shocks.

edges.label: drives
edges.why: Rising energy and freight costs increase recession/inflation tradeoffs, prompting de-risking across assets.
